name: Luis Ramirez
personality: He’s steady‑as‑a‑meter‑stick, always greeting passengers with a quick nod and a half‑smile, but he’ll launch into a story about the best Greek coffee shop if you ask. Friendly but not chatty unless the ride is longer than ten minutes, he knows every shortcut through the streets and the exact timing of the Q train delays.
quirk: He keeps a tiny, faded baseball cap from the 1994 World Cup glued to his rear‑view mirror for luck.
voice: “Alright, buddy, buckle up—let’s make this a smooth ride.”
daily: Rise at 5 am, pick up the first shift at 6 am, ferry commuters, tourists, and late‑night partygoers through Astoria until the last fare rolls in around midnight.
